Levels.L1: Logs, categorises and prioritises incidents accurately. Resolves routine incidents within agreed SLAs with support from senior analysts. Escalates appropriately when stuck.
Levels.L2: Resolves the majority of incidents independently within SLA. Recognises trends across tickets and flags them. Provides clear updates to affected users.
Levels.L3: Resolves complex and cross-system incidents independently. Coordinates response to major incidents (e.g. portal outage, mail flow issue) and communicates clearly with the business throughout.
Levels.L4: Leads major incident response, coordinating across IT and the business. Drives post-incident reviews and ensures lessons are turned into improvements. Sets and tracks SLAs for the team.
Levels.L5: Sets the AGI standard for incident management practice. Mentors leads on major incident response. Improves underlying systems and processes so incidents recur less often.

Levels.L1: Fulfils routine requests (access, equipment, software) using documented procedures. Keeps tickets up to date and the requester informed.
Levels.L2: Fulfils a broad range of requests independently, including joiner/mover/leaver processes (asset return on termination, account deactivation). Handles software installation, internet site unblock, and teleworking access requests using AGI's authorisation flow. Spots requests that should become a standard catalogue item.
Levels.L3: Handles complex or unusual requests and proposes process improvements. Owns parts of the service catalogue, ensuring procedures are accurate and current.
Levels.L4: Owns the service catalogue and request management process. Ensures common requests are streamlined and automated where possible.
Levels.L5: Sets the AGI direction for request management. Drives automation and self-service to reduce manual handling.

Levels.L1: Uses basic troubleshooting techniques and AGI’s knowledge base to diagnose common issues. Knows when to escalate.
Levels.L2: Applies a systematic approach to diagnose issues across endpoint, network, identity and core applications. Uses logs and diagnostic tools effectively.
Levels.L3: Diagnoses complex, cross-system issues independently. Helps less senior analysts develop their troubleshooting approach.
Levels.L4: Coaches the team on advanced troubleshooting. Steps in for the hardest diagnostic problems and ensures the team is learning from each one.
Levels.L5: Identifies systemic causes behind recurring issues and works with engineering or vendors to address them at the source.

Levels.L1: Provisions, supports and decommissions standard endpoints (laptops, mobiles) following documented procedures. Knows AGI's Clear Desk and Clear Screen policy, including the 15-minute auto-lock requirement.
Levels.L2: Confident across the full endpoint lifecycle, including asset return on termination. Supports operating system, browser, and standard productivity tools (Microsoft 365, etc.). Handles mobile device and teleworking access requests in line with AGI's Mobile Device and Technology Policy (off-premises authorisation, encryption requirements, CTO-issued remote access certificate). Recognises and handles common security issues on endpoints.
Levels.L3: Owns parts of the endpoint estate. Manages device policy in MDM/Intune (or equivalent), patches, and standard images. Proposes improvements to the endpoint experience.
Levels.L4: Owns AGI’s endpoint standards and tooling. Drives improvements to security posture, deployment speed and user experience.
Levels.L5: Sets endpoint strategy across AGI. Balances security, productivity and cost. Evaluates new technologies and pilots them where appropriate.

Levels.L1: Carries out routine identity tasks (account creation, group membership, password resets) following documented procedures. Knows and applies AGI's Password Policy (minimum 8 characters with mixed case, numeric and special characters; no reuse; 3-monthly rotation; not stored in browser or macro; not shared).
Levels.L2: Confident with joiner/mover/leaver processes, MFA, and standard access requests. Understands and follows AGI’s least-privilege principles.
Levels.L3: Owns parts of the identity estate. Manages role definitions, conditional access policies, and access reviews. Identifies and remediates access risks.
Levels.L4: Owns AGI’s identity practice. Ensures access reviews, joiner/mover/leaver processes, and privileged access management are operating effectively.
Levels.L5: Sets identity strategy across AGI. Drives zero-trust and least-privilege practices. Ensures alignment with compliance requirements.

Levels.L1: Supports core productivity and business applications (Microsoft 365, Teams, line-of-business tools) for standard user issues.
Levels.L2: Supports a broad range of AGI applications including claims and policy admin systems. Liaises with vendors and internal engineering when needed.
Levels.L3: Acts as the service desk expert for one or more business applications. Manages vendor escalations and works closely with engineering on application incidents.
Levels.L4: Owns service desk capability for key business applications. Ensures the team has the knowledge and tools to support them well, and that engineering and vendor relationships are healthy.
Levels.L5: Sets AGI’s direction for application support, including which applications are supported by the desk vs. engineering vs. vendor.

Levels.L1: Familiar with core ITIL concepts (incident, request, change, problem) at AGI and how they apply day to day.
Levels.L2: Applies ITIL practices consistently. Recognises when a recurring incident should become a problem record, or when a request should follow change management.
Levels.L3: Champions ITIL practice within the team. Identifies process gaps and proposes improvements (e.g. better change communication, clearer SLAs).
Levels.L4: Owns AGI’s service management practice for the team. Drives process improvements across incident, request, change, problem and knowledge.
Levels.L5: Sets AGI’s service management strategy. Ensures the practice is fit for AGI’s scale and is delivering business value, not bureaucracy.

Levels.L1: Uses the knowledge base to resolve tickets. Suggests improvements when articles are unclear or missing.
Levels.L2: Regularly contributes new articles and updates existing ones. Writes in clear, accessible language for end users.
Levels.L3: Owns a section of the knowledge base. Curates articles for accuracy, encourages contributions from the team, and tracks article effectiveness.
Levels.L4: Owns AGI’s knowledge management practice for the team. Ensures the knowledge base reduces ticket volume and accelerates resolution.
Levels.L5: Sets AGI’s knowledge strategy, including self-service for end users.

Levels.L1: Understands and follows AGI's Information Security Management System (ISMS) and Privacy Policy. Aware of the four-tier Information Classification Policy and applies it to the data the service desk handles (member, claims, financial, TFN). Recognises and reports suspected phishing and security events to security.services@agigroup.com.au.
Levels.L2: Confidently handles security-related tickets (account lockouts, suspicious activity, lost devices). Performs basic phishing triage. Follows incident reporting procedures.
Levels.L3: Acts as a first responder for many security events. Coaches the team in recognising and handling them. Works with the cybersecurity function on improvements.
Levels.L4: Owns the service desk’s contribution to AGI’s security posture. Ensures the team is trained and equipped to handle security events well.
Levels.L5: Sets AGI’s service desk security practice as part of the overall information security strategy. Drives improvements based on threat intelligence and incidents.

Levels.L1: Communicates clearly, patiently and professionally with end users at all levels. Sets realistic expectations and follows up.
Levels.L2: Communicates well even in stressful situations. Adapts style to the user (e.g. claims staff vs. executive). Provides clear status updates during outages.
Levels.L3: Sets the tone for how the team communicates with the business. Coaches less senior analysts on difficult conversations and updates during incidents.
Levels.L4: Owns end-user communications standards for the team, including during major incidents. Builds strong relationships with key business stakeholders.
Levels.L5: Sets AGI’s standard for end-user communication during incidents and change. Owns relationships with senior business stakeholders.

Levels.L1: Displays a positive and enthusiastic attitude towards learning and developing new skills, taking initiative to contribute where possible. Accepts feedback graciously and shows resilience in the face of challenges. Eager to help out with whatever needs doing.
Levels.L2: Demonstrates a can-do attitude, taking on complex tasks with confidence. Takes initiative to contribute to the team. Shows adaptability when facing change and maintains an open mindset.
Levels.L3: Maintains a positive attitude even in stressful situations. Leads by example, showing resilience and a solutions-oriented approach to problems.
Levels.L4: Demonstrates an uplifting attitude that motivates team members. Consistently solutions-oriented and aware of their position as a leader within the team. Encourages others to do the same.
Levels.L5: Exhibits a forward-thinking attitude, viewing challenges as opportunities for growth. Inspires team members across multiple teams. Capable of addressing morale issues in their part of IT.

Levels.L1: Responsive, pleasant and proactive across all channels. Work is usually presented clearly so others can understand. Verbal and written communication is easy to follow.
Levels.L2: Consistently responsive and clear in communication. Work is consistently presented in a way that others understand.
Levels.L3: Communicates clearly and concisely in written and verbal form, on both technical and non-technical subjects, in an audience-oriented way. Actively listens to others and pays attention to non-verbal cues.
Levels.L4: Presents work with polish and confidence. Leads team meetings effectively, internally and externally. Growing skill at conflict resolution. Written communication is clear, compelling and easy to follow.
Levels.L5: Fosters a culture of clear, audience-oriented communication across several teams. Capable of influencing and persuading others at any level of AGI, including the executive team. Coaches others to uplift their written, verbal and interpersonal skills.

Levels.L1: Actively participates in team events, contributing ideas during planning and retros. Shows eagerness to learn from more experienced team members. Conducts themselves in line with AGI's Code of Conduct.
Levels.L2: Consistently engages in team activities and discussions, stepping up to provide expertise and raising issues or asking for clarification when needed. Voluntarily takes on additional tasks.
Levels.L3: Fosters a collaborative team environment, encouraging active participation. Takes initiative to lead technical discussions and decision-making, explaining concepts and clarifying misunderstandings.
Levels.L4: Encourages and enables team members to contribute. Challenges assumptions and encourages a diversity of views to surface misunderstandings. Suggests additional sessions where they help the team align.
Levels.L5: Facilitates engagement across multiple teams by organising regular events, workshops, and team-building activities. Promotes a culture of learning and sharing.

Levels.L1: Understands their area of work and shares their knowledge frequently with their teammates.
Levels.L2: Understands their work domain, shares their knowledge frequently and contributes to team documentation. Watches out for opportunities to share what they know.
Levels.L3: Understands their team's domain and contributes actively to documentation. Watches out for opportunities to share knowledge and encourages others to do the same.
Levels.L4: Fosters a culture of documentation and knowledge sharing within their team and with their business stakeholders; actively demonstrates these behaviours.
Levels.L5: Fosters this culture across several teams and their respective business stakeholders.

Levels.L1: Helps teammates when requested. Gives or shares credit where due.
Levels.L2: When requested, helps teammates overcome obstacles, resolve blockers, and complete work. Shares credit where due.
Levels.L3: Often helps teammates overcome obstacles, resolve blockers, and complete work. Shares credit where due.
Levels.L4: Consistently and proactively helps teammates overcome obstacles, resolve blockers, and complete work. Shares credit where due.
Levels.L5: Consistently works across teams to help them resolve blockers and complete work. Ensures credit is shared and given where due.

Levels.L1: Understands how to deliver praise and constructive feedback in a useful way. Learns and improves from feedback provided by others.
Levels.L2: Delivers praise and constructive feedback to their team, teammates and manager. Actively seeks feedback to learn and improve.
Levels.L3: Delivers feedback to teammates and to business stakeholders when opportunities arise. Actively seeks 360-degree feedback to learn and improve.
Levels.L4: Fosters a culture of delivering praise and constructive feedback within their team and with business stakeholders. Actively demonstrates these behaviours. Proactively seeks 360-degree feedback.
Levels.L5: Fosters this culture across several teams as well as their respective business stakeholders. Promotes a continual improvement mindset in which feedback is appreciated as a facilitator of professional growth.

Levels.L1: Understands the impact of bias on decision making. Understands what accountability means.
Levels.L2: Strives to be objective and reflects on their own biases when making decisions. Holds themselves accountable for outcomes.
Levels.L3: Strives to be objective and reflects on biases when making decisions. Holds themselves accountable for outcomes and helps teammates do the same.
Levels.L4: Takes ownership of decisions made in their team by helping teammates make clear decisions aligned with AGI's goals, backing those decisions, and taking responsibility for their success. Raises awareness of how bias impacts decisions and ensures accountability is practised within their team.
Levels.L5: Takes ownership of decisions made across several teams, helping them make clear decisions aligned with AGI's goals. Raises awareness of bias and ensures accountability is practised throughout those teams.

Levels.L1: Contributes to conversations about AGI's strategy and principles when appropriate. Goal-oriented and works towards their team's goals.
Levels.L2: Has conversations about strategy and principles with teammates when appropriate. Goal-oriented and works towards the team's goals.
Levels.L3: Has conversations about strategy with teammates to ensure team alignment. Ensures their team is continuously working towards shared goals.
Levels.L4: Fosters a culture within their team of having conversations based on AGI's strategy and principles to create alignment. Ensures the team is continuously working towards its goals.
Levels.L5: Fosters this culture across several teams. Ensures several teams are continuously working towards shared goals.

Levels.L1: Understands their team's practices and processes.
Levels.L2: Sometimes thinks about team practices and processes and discusses improvements with the team.
Levels.L3: Regularly thinks about team practices and processes and discusses improvements with the team.
Levels.L4: Thinks about team practices and processes and regularly discusses improvements with their team. Sometimes collaborates with others to improve practices across IT.
Levels.L5: Thinks about practices and processes that affect several teams, discusses improvements with the right parties, and drives implementation. Usually collaborates with others on improving practices across IT.

Levels.L1: Seeks out mentorship to grow their own experience.
Levels.L2: Seeks out mentorship. Sometimes mentors teammates in an open, respectful, flexible, empathetic manner.
Levels.L3: Mentors teammates in an open, respectful, flexible, empathetic manner. Seeks out mentoring opportunities specifically to create redundancy and backfill ability within the team.
Levels.L4: Mentors teammates and members of other teams as needed, with others actively seeking their feedback. Capable of driving initiatives to improve team productivity.
Levels.L5: Leads by example, never hesitating to get hands-on wherever needed. Implements processes and identifies opportunities for improvement. Fosters a mentoring culture across teams.

Levels.L1: Has a basic understanding of the services they support and the business areas that depend on them.
Levels.L2: Has a basic understanding of those services and how they contribute to AGI's overall business.
Levels.L3: Has a thorough understanding of those services and how they contribute to AGI's business. Has a basic understanding of adjacent business areas.
Levels.L4: Has a thorough understanding of the services they support, AGI's strategy, and how it maps to the wider group insurance market. Understands adjacent business areas and how they interact.
Levels.L5: Has a thorough understanding of several business domains within AGI and how they contribute to overall strategy.

Levels.L1: Demonstrates an understanding of basic industry trends and technologies relevant to their domain. Willing to learn and apply new technologies and processes. Capable of doing research to validate work.
Levels.L2: Actively stays up to date with industry trends and technologies relevant to their domain. Applies this knowledge to solve problems and improve existing systems.
Levels.L3: Has in-depth knowledge of industry best practices and emerging trends. Uses this knowledge to lead the team in developing innovative solutions.
Levels.L4: Leads research efforts to stay ahead of industry trends and technologies relevant to their domain. Applies this knowledge to planning and decision making.
Levels.L5: Maintains expert-level knowledge of industry trends and technologies. Influences the direction of projects and initiatives at AGI based on this knowledge.

Levels.L1: Understands AGI's technology strategy.
Levels.L2: Understands AGI's technology strategy and consistently applies it to their work.
Levels.L3: Understands AGI's technology strategy. Usually involved in discussions about implications for their team.
Levels.L4: Collaborates on their team's technology choices in line with AGI's strategy. Sometimes involved in work on the overall IT strategy.
Levels.L5: Usually involved in strategic IT decisions and plans. Leads cross-team strategic efforts, influencing decisions to achieve alignment on major goals.

Levels.L1: Asks good questions to understand what the user is trying to do, not just the symptom they reported. Understands the basic value of the services they support.
Levels.L2: Looks beyond individual tickets to spot patterns and improvement opportunities. Suggests small process or knowledge improvements.
Levels.L3: Thoroughly understands how IT services support the AGI business. Proposes improvements to specific services and contributes to their roadmap.
Levels.L4: Owns service improvement for the desk. Works with the business to understand what really matters to them and shapes services accordingly.
Levels.L5: Sets the service portfolio direction at AGI. Ensures IT services genuinely enable the business (e.g. supports new claims volumes, new portal capabilities, M&A integration) rather than just keeping the lights on.
